memoryArtifacts.headline: Deleted
memoryArtifacts.description:
Surely we can do better than this. My grandfather Ralph H. Chamberlain had this lineage documented back to Tancred of Tancorville. What happened to the family backwards of 1330?
Who deleted it? I'm go …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard III Chamberlain於1356年出生在英國奧可福爾爾本時，他的父親Sir Richard Chamberlain II46歲，母親Jane Reyns27歲。他和Margaret de Loveyne至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1396年8月24日在英國貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯享年40歲，埋葬在英國貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard III Chamberlain于1356年出生在英国奧可福爾爾本时，他的父亲Sir Richard Chamberlain II46岁，母亲Jane Reyns27岁。他和Margaret de Loveyne至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1396年8月24日在英国貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯享年40岁，埋葬在英国貝德福爾提爾斯沃斯。

nameMeanings.name: Chamberlain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: status name from Old French chambrelain, Norman French cambrelanc, cambrelen(c) ‘chamberlain’ (of ancient Germanic origin, from kamer ‘chamber, room’, Latin camera (see Chambers ) + the diminutive suffix -(l)ing). This was originally the name of an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, but is so widespread in late medieval England that it must sometimes have been used of people of more ordinary status, perhaps as a nickname for an officious or self-important person or for someone who played the role of chamberlain in a folk play, tableau, or ceremony. Compare Chancellor for a possible similar usage.

variants.name: Chamberlin
meanings: French: occupational name for an official in charge of the private chambers of his master, from Old French chamberlenc ‘chamberlain’. See also Chamberlain and compare above.

variants.name: Chambers
meanings: from Middle English chaumbre ‘room (in a house); reception room in a palace or official building’ (Old French chambre). It is identical in implied function with the Chamberlain , which denoted an official: to pay in cameram was to pay into the exchequer, of which the camerarius or chamberer was in charge. The surname also applied to clerks employed there. As the office of Chamberlain rose in the social scale, this term remained reserved for more humble servants of the bedchamber or private quarters.
(of Norman origin): habitational name from Les Chambres, Manche (France).

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard III Chamberlain was born in 1356, in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England, his father, Sir Richard Chamberlain II, was 46 and his mother, Jane Reyns, was 27. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Margaret de Loveyne. He died on 24 August 1396, in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England, at the age of 40, and was buried in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard III Chamberlain est né en 1356, à Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard Chamberlain II, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Jane Reyns, avait 27 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Margaret de Loveyne. Il est décédé le 24 août 1396, à Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans, et a été enterré à Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard III Chamberlain 1356 in Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard Chamberlain II 46 und seine Mutter Jane Reyns 27. Er hatte mit Margaret de Loveyne mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb am 24. August 1396 im Alter von 40 Jahren in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England und wurde in Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard III Chamberlain è nato il nel 1356 nel Shirburn, Oxfordshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard Chamberlain II, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Jane Reyns, aveva 27 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Margaret de Loveyne. Morì il 24 agosto 1396, nel Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England, all'età di 40 anni, e fu sepolto a Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard III Chamberlainが1356年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアシュイルブルンで生まれた時，父Sir Richard Chamberlain IIは46歳，母Jane Reynsは27歳であった。彼はMargaret de Loveyneとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1396年8月24日にイギリスベドフォードシュアイアティルスウオルスで40歳で死去し，イギリスベドフォードシュアイアティルスウオルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard III Chamberlain(이)가 1356년 영국 스퍼드실르 실르번에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard Chamberlain II(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Jane Reyns(은)는 27세였다. 그는 Margaret de Loveyne(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1396년 8월 24일 영국 베드퍼드실르 틸스워스에서 40세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 베드퍼드실르 틸스워스에 묻혔다.

mn.Stanbridge, Bedfordshire, England, Unite: Stanbridge, Beadafordscīr, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard III Chamberlain nasceu em 1356, em Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard Chamberlain II, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Jane Reyns, tinha 27 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha com Margaret de Loveyne. Ele faleceu em 24 de agosto de 1396, em Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, com 40 anos, e foi sepultado em Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard III Chamberlain родился в 1356 г. в местности ширбурн, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard Chamberlain II было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Jane Reyns, 27. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Margaret de Loveyne. Он умер 24 августа 1396 г. в местности тилсворт, бедфордшир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет и был похоронен в местности тилсворт, бедфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard III Chamberlain nació en 1356, en Shirburn, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard Chamberlain II, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Jane Reyns, tenía 27 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Margaret de Loveyne. Murió el 24 de agosto de 1396, en Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 40 años, y fue sepultado en Tilsworth, Bedfordshire, Inglaterra.
